For\nloading the library and making it a perfect layout for the bot,\nit takes O(n) time complexity. And for the training model\nand to predict the correct reply to the user\u2019s question, the\nalgorithm\u2019s running time cost O(n), and as we know for\nmaking the training model and putting different layers on it\nto make a perfect neural network, time complexity will be -\nO(4h(3d+h+d)) where the neural network layers d, h and it\nis the proposed time complexity for the LSTM algorithm.\nTherefore, the total time complexity (along with training\nmodel): - O(n+(4h(3d+h+d))) where h, d denotes layers and\nonly for layout and put predict replies,\n\n- Total time complexity: - O(n)\n- Space complexity: - O(n)\n\nFIGURE 18. Accuracy vs testing cases.\n\nAccording to TABLE 5, after performing, different meth-\nods on our chatbot or making the training model using a\ndifferent approach, we can see the different accuracy on each\ndifferent method. Here, LSTM in the case of single-layer\nand for testing and training approaches the time complexity\nwhere, in the case of RNN, the time complexity is the same,\nbut in the case of the decision tree, the time complexity\ndepends on the depth of the tree. Moreover, in the case of\naccuracy, the highest accuracy is from LSTM. The LSTM\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 128479\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nTABLE 5. Accuracy and complexity comparison.\n\nused different gates and also used a gradient to work as a\nneural network, or we can say a better RNN with a sequential\nnetwork. In the case of RNN, it stores sequential input and\nperforms suitable operations when it is needed as sequential\ndata output. In addition, the last decision tree makes different\nnodes and sub-node, and each node performs to store data\nfor users\u2019 use. Here, according to test cases and training\ncases, LSTM, then RNN, and lastly, the Decision Tree got\nthe highest accuracy.\n\nFIGURE 19. Graphical representation of accuracy in different methods.\n\nHowever, according to Fig. 19, we can see that with\ndifferent methods the accuracy increases differently. LSTM\nhas the highest accuracy, whereas RNN is close to LSTM\u2019s\naccuracy. However, the decision tree has the lowest accuracy\namong these three methods. Therefore, we can say that for\nour proposed model and training model, the best approach to\nuse is LSTM. The rest of the two methods are also the best\napproaches to different proposed models. However, as for\nour accuracy in the case of LSTM. Therefore, we choose\nthe LSTM approach to make neural network layers and also\nmake our training model to get the best results in cases of\ninteraction with users and getting the perfect or expected\nreply from a bot.\n\nIn the following table, we have outlined some existing\napproaches to our chatbot. Based on this comparison, it can\nbe clearly seen that our proposed model approaches are more\nrobust with respect to inter-user communication as well as\n\nTABLE 6. Comparison in terms of interface and other properties.\n\nproviding a quicker, more accurate, and more understandable\nresponse time.\n\nVI. APPLICATIONS\nA. PERSONALIZED CONVERSATIONS\nOur chatbot, Kiwi will provide personalized conversations\nwhere the users will be able to gain information and at the\nsame time, it will feel like a natural experience to them. The\nchatbot will be able to show empathy and be able to perform\npersonalized word choices and adjust its language and tone\nbased on the context of the conversation.\n\nB. OMNI-CHANNEL\nOur chatbot Kiwi can broadcast information across different\nmajor channels including government websites and impor-\ntant organizations, such As WHO which contain relevant\ninformation about Covid 19. If the user provides identifying\ninformation such as the composition of medicines, hospital\nor doctor requirements, the chatbot will provide all the nec-\nessary details and even provide links to websites containing\nthe required information by the user.\n\nC. ACCESSIBILITY\nThe chatbot interface is accessible to all users regardless\nof their platform. The users only need a stable internet\n\n128480 VOLUME 11, 2023\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nconnection to be able to use it. It can read the information\naloud if the user prefers to hear the information which he or\nneeds. It is helpful for the visually impaired as they can speak\nto the chatbot about their queries and the chatbot, in turn,\nspeaks to them and provides important information.\n\nD. CONTEXTUAL ASSISTANCE\nThe chatbot can also be a source of contextual assistance to\nthe health officials and organizations for updating informa-\ntion about Covid 19. We can get a better understanding of the\nproblems, by troubleshooting customer issues and the work\nfor the next updates can be streamlined with the necessary\nimprovements that need to be undertaken.\n\nE. BOOKINGS\nThe chatbot will provide all the necessary information about\nthe availability of hospital beds in an area where the user\nwants the patient to be taken. It would also provide the contact\nnumber for the hospitals and the ambulances, so the booking\nof hospital beds would be easier for the user as they would not\nhave to travel long distances to get a hospital bed. Users can\nalso book an appointment with the doctor if they feel they are\ninfected and need advice right from the safety of their homes.\nThe contact numbers for the doctors will be provided by the\nchatbot and the users don\u2019t have to get out of their homes to\ngather the information.\n\nF. CONVERSATIONAL SERVICE\nThe chatbot will be able to provide conversational service for\nany hospital or health organization by conversing with the\npatients, and it would be helpful in optimizing the treatment\nprocedure based on the reactions of the patients. The medica-\ntions can be selected and the people can also be made aware\nof the dangers posed to them if they fail to take the necessary\nprecautions.\n\nG. STUDENT ASSISTANCE\nStudents conducting research on COVID-19 or the use of\ntechnology in medical science to treat COVID-19 can find\nuseful information from the chatbot. They would find out the\naccuracy level required to maintain a stable conversation and\nprovide the correct information with respect to the queries\ngiven by the user.\n\nVII. BENEFITS\nX 24/7 Support: Chatbots are always at an advantage when\n\nit comes to all-around support even without having to\nscale your team. The chatbot will be available to offer\ninstant and accurate responses around the clock without\nthe need to take any breaks, as is the case with human\ndeployment.\n\nX Save Resources: Chatbots are always useful in saving\nthe time of operators as well as the users. The users\ndo not have to spend their time searching for health\ncenters or hospitals to get information about Covid 19.\n\nIt will also be cost-effective as deploying a chatbot saves\nmoney for any organization since they do not need to pay\nfor the manpower required to maintain the equivalent\nfacilities for the users. It also saves the user money as\nhe does not need to use any transportation and travel\nanywhere to inquire about his needs.\n\nX Reduce Stress for users: It is generally observed that\npatients avoid getting in contact with the customer ser-\nvice of hospitals. They are more comfortable if they are\ntalking to a chatbot and getting their information. The\nsurvey conducted by Help shift discovered that 79% of\npeople prefer live chat to other channels and 55% would\nchoose to use a chatbot if it was available. Chatbots are\nwidely considered to be a quicker and more efficient\nsolution to the users\u2019 problems.\n\nX Handling Information: Chatbots are helpful at handling\nhuge amounts of information and providing the correct\ninformation for the given query without wasting any\ntime. Our chatbot, Kiwi is trained in such a way that it\nhandles all the information given to it seamlessly and\nprovides it as an output to the user\u2019s queries with a high\nrate of accuracy.\n\nFIGURE 20. The chatbot replies regarding medicines that helps the user\nto determine his next course of action.\n\nIn the given Fig. 20, the user asks the chatbot about what\nmedicines he can take if he is infected. The chatbot provides\nall the information about the available medications and thera-\npies for Covid 19 along with their advantages and side effects.\nTherefore, the user gets a full description of his query andwill\nbe able to decide his next course of action after going through\nthe information given to him.\nX Instant response: Chatbots are generally known for their\n\nability to respond to any query immediately, and they can\nhandle queries from thousands of users at the same time\nand provide information to the users immediately. The\nsimultaneous answers provided by our chatbot, Kiwi\nwould also help us to analyze the average response time\nand improve its activities so that the users can get an even\nmore advanced system at their hands.\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 128481\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nVIII. CONCLUSION\nFrom this paper, we can conclude that the chatbot is very\neasy to use for all people; they can use this chatbot in their\nlanguage. This bot offers medical-related information like\ndoctor\u2019s contact details, address of nearby hospitals, contact\ndetails for getting an oxygen cylinder, about the disease its\nsymptoms, its prevalence, diagnosis, and its treatment pro-\ncedures. We consider that our findings will help researchers\nto take advantage of the advanced information in the layout\nand other stuff of these innovative technologies, which may\nbe required for continuous development in the functionality\nof medical chatbots and may help prevent COVID 19. This\nmedical chatbot has wide future opportunities. People in\nremote areas can also receive benefits from this. Here we use\n\u2018TensorFlow\u2019, which helps to build the NLP for chatbots and\nutilizes deep neural network architecture. After building the\nnetwork for our chatbot, it will predict the correct answers\nto the user\u2019s queries. Even if it is not in the training model,\nthe bot tries to predict it closer by checking the sentences\nand their words which will be closer to the training model\u2019s\nresponse.\n\nCOMPLIANCE WITH ETHICAL STANDARDS\nCONFLICT OF INTEREST\nThe authors declare that they have no conflict of interest.\n\nFUNDING\nThere is no external funding involved in this research.\n\nSUPPLEMENTARY\nhttps://github.com/Kodierer-desafiador/Covid-19-dataset-\njson-file\n\nREFERENCES\n[1] J.               "snippet": "\ue200filecite\ue202turn0file0\ue201\n\nReceived 17 November 2022, accepted 2 December 2022, date of publication 6 December 2022,\ndate of current version 13 December 2022.\n\nDigital Object Identifier 10.1109/ACCESS.2022.3227208\n\nAn AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for\nInfectious Disease Prediction\nSANJAY CHAKRABORTY1, HRITHIK PAUL 2, SAYANI GHATAK 2, SAROJ KUMAR PANDEY3,\nANKIT KUMAR 3, KAMRED UDHAM SINGH 4, AND MOHD ASIF SHAH 5,6\n1Computer Science and Engineering Department, Techno International Newtown, Kolkata 700156, India\n2Computer Science and Engineering Department, JIS University, Kolkata 700109, India\n3Department of Computer Engineering and Applications, GLA University, Mathura 281406, India\n4Department of Computer Science and Information Engineering, National Cheng Kung University, Tainan 701, Taiwan\n5Department of Economics, Kebri Dehar University, Kebri Dehar 250, Ethiopia\n6School of Business, Woxsen University, Hyderabad 502345, India\n\nCorresponding author: Mohd Asif Shah (drmohdasifshah@kdu.edu.et)\n\nABSTRACT The purpose of this paper is to show concisely how we can promote chatbots in the medical\nsector and cure infectious diseases. We can create awareness through the users and the users can get\nproper medical solutions to prevent disease. We created a preliminary training model and a study report\nto improve human interaction in databases in 2021. Through natural language processing, we describe the\nhuman behaviors and characteristics of the chatbot. In this paper, we propose an AI Chatbot interaction\nand prediction model using a deep feedforward multilayer perceptron. Our analysis discovered a gap in\nknowledge about theoretical guidelines and practical recommendations for creating AI chatbots for lifestyle\nimprovement programs. A brief comparison of our proposed model concerning the time complexity and\naccuracy of testing is also discussed in this paper. In our work, the loss is a minimum of 0.1232 and the\nhighest accuracy is 94.32%. This study describes the functionalities and possible applications of medical\nchatbots and explores the accompanying challenges posed by the use of these emerging technologies during\nsuch health crises mainly posed by pandemics. We believe that our findings will help researchers get a better\nunderstanding of the layout and applications of these revolutionary technologies, which will be required for\ncontinuous improvement in medical chatbot functionality and will be useful in avoiding COVID-19.\n\nINDEX TERMS Artificial intelligence, chatbot, LSTM algorithm, machine learning, natural language\nprocessing, query processing.\n\nI. INTRODUCTION\nCovid-19 is an ailment because of the SARS-CoV-2 virus.\n\u2018World Health Organization (WHO)\u2019 has been declared a\npandemic on March 11, 2020. Around 15 million human\nbeings have been affected worldwide by more than one mil-\nlion deaths by covid-19. From the start, the affordability\nand sustainability of oxygen have been a problem in poor\nand underdeveloped countries. Oxygen is a very important\nmedicine for treating hospitalized patients with Covid-19.\nAccording to PATH, the organization that works with global\ninstitutions and businesses to tackle health problems, the\ndemand for Oxygen cylinders has been growing between\n6%-8% eacdaily India. When someone gets severe Covid-19,\n\nThe associate editor coordinating the review of this manuscript and\n\napproving it for publication was Shadi Alawneh .\n\nthe oxygen levels in the body get low. Patients suffered from\nenormous fevers, coughs, and lost sense of taste and smell, all\nof which became problematic when the SARS-CoV-2 virus\ninvaded them. As a result, to prevent such massive prob-\nlems associated with COVID-19 and facilitate a quick cure\nmethod, we have developed a Chatbot that facility an inter-\nconnection between users and computers in a natural manner.\nIn the twenty-first century, artificial intelligence algorithms\nhave been used to create a revolutionary medium with which\nusers can interact with their needs to prevent and resolve\nacoustic problems easily; one of these is chatbots, a modern\nform of interaction. Chatbot systems use human interactions\nsuch as decision-making performing daily tasks, replying to\nusers quickly, and solving problems like a human. RELATED WORK\nIn the paper [1] the author described the whole procedure\nof developing a chatbot by dividing the process into seg-\nments such as speech-to-text conversion, natural language\nprocessing, response generation, knowledge base creation,\ndialogue management, text-to-speech, etc. The author has\nalso included security considerations such as security flaws\nin chatbot platforms and malicious chatbots. In the paper [3]\nthe authors reviewed topics surrounding the chatbot\u2019s knowl-\nedge domain, response generation, text processing, machine\n\nlearning model, and the dataset usage and evaluation strategy\ntopics. In the paper [4] the authors have described the building\nof a chatbot that can provide an authentic and accurate answer\nfor any type of query using Artificial Intelligence Markup\nLanguage (AIML) and Latent Semantic Analysis (LSA) by\nusing the application of the python platform. In the paper [5]\nthe author covers different approaches to chatbot develop-\nment, including key points on chatbot integration and deploy-\nment, and employs machine learning to configure a chatbot.\nIn the paper [2] the author discussed the method of data anal-\nysis, which allows the analytical system to learn through the\nway of solving problems and applying similar methods in its\nworking process. In the paper [6] the authors have analyzed\nhow artificial intelligence and machine learning are imple-\nmented in popular use to make advancements in chatbot ser-\nvices specifically in helping users to access college websites.\nThe problem selects the closest matching response from the\nclosest matching statement that matches the input and then\nchooses a response from an available selection of statements\nfor that response. In the paper, [7] the author has elaborated on\nthe use of diverse neural framework exhibits as the learning\ntechnique for setting up the chatbot to make it continuously\nlike human enlistment authority. NLP techniques such as\nNLTK (python) can be applied for speech analysis and intel-\nligent responses can be generated by designing a model to\nprovide appropriate human-like responses. In the paper [8]\nthe authors have discussed their research on how to design,\ndevelop and evaluate a health assistant chatbot application\nthat helps users to ask any personal query related to health-\ncare without physically availing any hospital facilities. In the\npaper by [9] the authors have described an AI chatbot whose\nwork depends on Natural language processing. The chatbot\nusers can upload their queries related to healthcare without\nphysically availing of any healthcare facilities. It uses Google\nAPI for voice-text and text-voice conversations. The query is\nsent to the chatbot and the related answer is displayed on an\nandroid app. The system\u2019s main concern behind developing\nthis web-based platform is analyzing customers\u2019 sentiments.\nIn the paper [10] the authors have described a proposed idea to\ncreate a system with AI which meets the user\u2019s requirements.\nThe AI can predict diseases based on symptoms and give\na list of available treatments. The system can also give the\ncomposition of themedicines and their prescribed uses. In our\nchatbot model, we have incorporated the symptoms of Covid\n19 since it is based on only this one disease currently. It also\nshows the list of medications and precautions that the users\nmight take if they are infected. In the paper [11] the authors\nhave focused to show the implementation of a retrieval-based\nchatbot with voice support. They have investigated other\nstanding chatbots and how it is useful in helping the patients\nto fetch all the necessary details about Covid 19. BACKGROUND LSTM\nLong-Short Term Memory (LSTM) is an artificial neural\nnetwork famous for its applications in Artificial Intelligence\nand deep learning. Using its four main gates, it is easy to\nsolve complex problems in the fields of machine translation,\nspeech recognition, input-output mapping, and neural net-\nworks. When it comes to learning specific patterns, LSTMs\nperform better than other types of neural networks. It is a\nsort of RNN (Recurrent Neural Network) that is commonly\nused to learn sequential data and mapping issues. As far as\nthe LSTM gates are concerned, they fall under four main\ncategories - forget gate, input gate, output gate, and cell gate.\nThe purpose of every gate is to perform a specific function\nthat has to be achieved.\n\u2022 Forget gate: It is answerable for deciding that info\nis unbroken for scheming the cell state and that isn\u2019t\nrelevant and might be discarded. The information from\nthe previous hidden state or previous cell can be stated\nas ht-1 and the information from the current cell can be\nstated as xt. Two inputs are given in the forget gate.\n\n\u2022 Input gate: Input Gate comes in available in updating\nthe cell state and decides which records are necessary\nand which are not. It helps to discard the data, and the\ninput gate helps to discover necessary information and\nstore certain data in the relevant memory. ht-1 and xt\nare the inputs that are each passed via sigmoid and tanh\nfunctions respectively. \u2018tanh\u2019 regulates the network.\n\n\u2022 Output gate: The output gate decides what the next\nhidden state should be. It is the last gate. ht-1 and xt\nare exceeded to a sigmoid function. The most current\nmodified state is passed via the tanh function and is\nmultiplied with the sigmoid output to determine the\ninformation of hidden state has to carry.\n\n\u2022 Cell gate: First, all knowledge gained is accustomed to\ncalculating new cell states. Firstly, it increased with the\noutput. This has a chance of dropping values within this\nstate if it is increased by close values of zero.\n\nOur chatbot\u2019s interface is made with the Tkinter library.\nFor loading the library and making it a perfect layout for\nthe bot, it takes O(n) time complexity. However, for the\ntraining model and to predict the correct reply to the user\u2019s\nquestion, the algorithm\u2019s running time cost is O(n), and as\nwe know for making the training model and putting different\nlayers on it to make a perfect neural network, the time com-\nplexity is O(4h(3d+h+d)) where the neural network layers\nd, h and it is the proposed time complexity for the LSTM\nalgorithm.\n\nFIGURE 1. Functioning of Forget gate in LSTM algorithm.\n\nFIGURE 2. Functioning of the input gate in LSTM algorithm.\n\nA. WORKING WITH LSTM\nLSTM functions in the same manner as we have discussed in\nthe given figures. The inputs given to our model are stored\nin the memory of the neural network and during the training\nof the model, it cross-checks the present information with all\nthe accumulated ones and then finally provides the required\noutput. The algorithm has acted as a good classifier for the\nmodel inputs as it filters data from previous timestamps and\nsends the refurbished data to its memory. The collected data\nis sent to the output gate after it has passed through the forget\ngate and the prediction for the next output gets ready in time.\nThis helps in the precision of the model and reduces the\nspace complexity. Fig.1 to Fig4 describe the functionality of\ncomponents of LSTM method.\n\nIn Fig 5, the graph demonstrates the trend that we can\nsee during the testing phase of our model. The model is\ntrained with different types of input values. When a test set is\ngiven as an input, it predicts the upcoming inputs and shows\nthe output after analyzing them with the recent past values.\nAs the graph shows, the predicted values according to the\ncorresponding test set are quite accurate, and the model can\nmaintain consistency in its outputs with sensational precision.\n\nB. RECURRENT NEURAL NETWORK\nRNN stands for Recurrent Neural Network. RNN contains\ninternal memory, which makes it one of a kind since there is\n\n128472 VOLUME 11, 2023\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nFIGURE 3. Functioning of output gate in LSTM algorithm.\n\nFIGURE 4. Functioning of the cell gate in the LSTM algorithm.\n\nFIGURE 5. Graph showing test set versus predicted values using LSTM in\nour model.\n\nno other neural network possessing such functionality. RNN\nis a robust and one of the most promising algorithms. Due to\nthe presence of internal memory, RNNs can remember and\nreconstruct the inputs given to them, and they can predict\nthe upcoming inputs with great precision and efficiency. The\nuse of RNNs in popular usage has been described by Lex\n\nFridman as \u2018\u2018Whenever there is a sequence of data and that the\ntemporal dynamics that connect the data is more important\nthan the spatial content of each frame.\u2019\u2019 The working of RNN\nresembles that of a human brain. They use their predictive\noutput with seamless precision and provide the exact required\ninformation. To simply state the working of RNN, we can say\nthat recurrent neural networks. It works on two inputs, one\nis the recent past, and another is the present. It is the main\naspect because the sequence of data contains details that are\ncrucial to what will be coming up next. In our model, the\nRNN algorithm comes with this uniqueness since it analyzes\nthe recent past and present inputs according to the queries\ngiven by the user. Every time, the accuracy of the model\nmaintains the saturation level as the correct information is\ngiven as output and the model can detect what information\nthe user is likely to require next.\n\nFIGURE 6. The working procedure of the general RNN algorithm.\n\nIn Fig.6, the working of the general RNN algorithm is\nshown, where it can be seen that the algorithm works with an\nefficient process flow in which the input is sent to a hidden\nlayer that stores the information of the recent past and then,\nin turn, it produces it in the output layer after analyzing it with\nthe present input. This saves a lot of time during the training\nphase and helps us to decrease the time complexity of our\nmodel.\n\nC. WORKING WITH RNN\nIn our model, sequential data has been provided during the\ntraining of the chatbot model, and the RNN algorithm has\nhelped segregate all the different kinds of queries given as\ninputs and provide the correct output, which is the informa-\ntion provided to the user. The two major issues regarding\nRNN have been dealt with efficiently in our model:\n\u2022 Exploding Gradients \u2013 Sometimes, the algorithm\nassigns unusually high importance to the weights, which\nresults in exploding gradients. We have reduced such\na possibility significantly by truncating and squashing\ngradients by providing all types of relevant weights and\ntraining the model with the inputs.\n\n\u2022 Vanishing Gradients - It can happen when the values\ngiven for a gradient become very small, and the model\nstops studying or takes time very much, increasing the\ntime complexity as a result. We have strictly maintained\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 128473\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nthe time complexity at O(n), and the accuracy reaches\nas high as 9.5 approximately. LSTM comes in handy\nin maintaining a steady gradient with all the relevant\nand recurring values held together, and it maintains the\naccuracy level at a constant.\n\nFIGURE 7. Demonstration of the RNN algorithm as applied to our chatbot\nmodel.\n\nIn Fig 7, the demonstration of RNN is shown as it is applied\nto our chatbot model. During working with several inputs,\nthere might exist a deeper network with a certain input layer,\nwhile others might have more than one hidden layer. All of\nthese are trained together inside the .json file and are trained\nin such a way that each hidden layer has its own set of\nweights and biases. This gives independence to each layer,\nso, it does not have to remember all the inputs. This increases\nthe efficiency of the model and the accuracy of the output\ngiven will have significant growth.\n\nD. DECISION TREE MODEL\nThe decision tree is used for categorization and regression\nproblems, but it is mainly used to solve classification prob-\nlems. Where internal nodes show the dataset features, branch\nshows the rules of decision, and leaf node shows the outcome.\nThere are two nodes present in the decision tree. They are\nthe Leaf node and the Decision node. Decision nodes decide\nhow the algorithm works and Leaf nodes show the output of\nthat decision and are connected with Decision nodes. We can\nsay that the decision tree is the graphical representation of\nall the solutions for all the problems under given conditions.\nIt looks like a tree structure. It has a root node, all the branches\nconnected with it, and leaf nodes connected with branches.\nWe used the CART algorithm tomake theDecision tree. Deci-\nsion trees commonly impersonate human wondering capacity\nwhile making decisions. From the very beginning, we have\nto choose the root node that fully contains the datasets. Then\nwe can locate the satisfactory attribute dataset with the use\nof Attribute Selection Measurement (ASM). Next, we have\nto split the best attribute dataset, which contains possible\nvalues for the best attribute dataset. We have to generate\nthe decision tree node and finally, we have to search the\nbest dataset for a solution until we reach the final possible\n\nsolution. We used the following two mathematical equations\nto solve the determinant of the decision tree.\n\nInformation Gain = Entropy(S)\u2212 [(Weighted Average)\n\n\u2217Entropy (each feature)]\n\nGini Index = 1\u22126jP2j (1)\n\nFrom the above equation we get 0.6753 accuracies and Time\nComplexity of O(nkd).\n\nIn Fig 8, a demonstration of the decision tree model as\nit is applied to our chatbot model. Suppose any user of our\nchatbot, is unaware about the Covid-19 and wants to decide\nwhether he needs to know about the symptoms, doctors, or the\noxygen help for the patient. For solving the problem, the\ndecision tree begins with the root node (Corona Virus by\nASM). The root node splits similarly into the subsequent\ndecision node (Symptoms, Doctor, Oxygen, Emergency Bed)\nand different leaf nodes primarily based on the corresponding\nlabels. The subsequent decision node is in addition split into\nthree decision nodes, and these are the ultimate leaf nodes\nof the previous decision node. Finally, the machine learning\ntraining module finds the final leaf node (Helpers).\n\nFIGURE 8. Demonstration of Decision Tree Model as it is applied to our\nchatbot model.\n\nHowever, the user of the chatbot wants to know about the\noxygen helpers. From the upper diagram, we can see how the\nDecision Tree Model solves a problem.\n\nIV. PROPOSED MODEL\nWhenever the user writes anything to the interface of the bot,\nthe bot will reply and answer the corresponding questions\nor someone greets people using \u2018\u2018good morning\u2019\u2019 or \u2018\u2018good\nevening\u2019\u2019, the bot will also greet them by saying \u2018\u2018good morn-\ning, it\u2019s a nice day\u2019\u2019. Even if someone asks for the time, the bot\neventually tells the user what time it is. Moreover, if the user\nasks some different queries to the bot, the bot also predicts\nthe most accurate queries for it. For example, if the user\nexpresses his/her emotions that are not in the training model,\nby saying, \u2018\u2018it\u2019s a bad day to me\u2019\u2019 the bot eventually replays\nby predicting the near, answers, \u2018\u2018I am here to help you, tell\n\n128474 VOLUME 11, 2023\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nme your problem \u2018\u2018. In this chatbot, \u2018\u2018voice recognition\u2019\u2019 is\nalso implemented as another feature. If the user wants to say\nsomething in voice instead of texting, the bot also replies to it\nin voice and in text, both modes. Here, before making the bot\nready, we have to train our bot using \u2018\u2018tags\u2019\u2019 and \u2018\u2018responses\u2019\u2019.\nAs an example, in the tag section, if we write \u2018\u2018greetings\u2019\u2019\nand in the response section, we write \u2018\u2018good morning\u2019\u2019, or \u2018\u2018it\nis a nice day\u2019\u2019, the bot eventually replies whenever it finds\nsomething closer to its response. With the machine-learning\nconcept and using NLP and TensorFlow, wemake predictions\nfor the bot what will be accurate answers after training it.\nHere, the training model will be the main part, because in\nthe training model all the training like what type of questions\nor queries the bot should be answered is implemented. The\nTensorFlow helps to build the NLP for chatbots and utilizes\ndeep neural network architecture after building the network\nfor our chatbot. The bot will predict the correct answers to\nusers\u2019 queries. Even if it is not in the training model, the bot\nalso tries to predict it closer by checking the sentences and its\nword, which will be closer to the training model\u2019s response.\nIn Fig.4, we can see that a user can freely decide what its\nmode will be. If the user chooses text mode, just to write its\nquery to the chatbot interface, the bot will make a prediction\nusing the training model, then reply and give a perfect reply\nto it. If the user chooses voice mode, the voice recognition\nmodule will recognize the voice and convert it to the text.\nThen the bot will do the same thing just for text mode and,\nin the bot, will reply to the query and give a speech about it.\nIn the end, if the user wants to continue, they can continue\nand if the user doesn\u2019t want to continue, can click on the exit\nbutton, then the chatbot will turn off. In the future, whenever\na user wants to use it, they can use it. In the case of predicting\nthe user\u2019s text and giving an accurate reply by the bot, the\nfollowing algorithm will represent how it is working, queries.\nThe bot will detect which type of query it is and predict the\nmost accurate answers for it.\n\nFig. 9 shows the block diagram or process flow diagram\nof our proposed chatbot system. In Alogirthm-1, we have\nto make a json file at first which will contain tags, question\npatterns, and its response. Then we have to load the json file\ninto the training file. After that, we have to make a list and\nalso make a list that will ignore unnecessary words. Then\nwhatever words are stored in the list, we have to \u2018\u2018lemmatize\u2019\u2019\nall of the stored words and then again store them in a new\nvariable, \u2018\u2018words\u2019\u2019 in the form of sorted. Thenwe have to open\na file in binary mode, \u2018\u2018words.pk1\u2019\u2019 and make a dump file\nof it. Again, we have to \u2018\u2018lemmatize\u2019\u2019 the patterns and store\nthe corresponding output in the training list. Then shuffle all\nelements present in the training list. Then store one part of\nthe training list in train_x variables and another part store\nin train_y variables. After that, we have to make a neural\nnetwork by adding Dense and Dropout. Then, compile it and\nsave it as \u2018\u2018chatbot_model.h5\u2019\u2019. After that whenever a user\nwrites something on chatbot interfaces, the bot will predict\nthe closer reply by using the training model. Even if the user\nwants to go on voice mode, they will hear the reply from\n\nFIGURE 9. Flowchart of the overall chatbot system.\n\nthe bot even though they can command the bot through their\nvoice, and the bot will convert the voice to text and do the\nsame work as it is done in text mode. In the case of making\nthe chatbot interface. Here the Tkinter library is used. Tkinter\ngives a good GUI. Where the chit-chat between the user and\nthe bot is done in the bot\u2019s interface, which is made in python\nusing the Tkinter library.\n\nV. RESULT ANALYSIS AND DISCUSSION\nIn order to fully understand our model and its results, it is\nnecessary to discuss the dataset that was used in the making\nof the model. The reason for this is that text-based predictions\nare needed to facilitate inter-communication with the user in\norder to solve their complex issue.\n\nA. DATASET DESCRIPTION\nThis dataset is in JSON format and has been incorporated into\nour model. We have included some keys which have been\nused for pattern recognition and input-output mapping. Key\nfactors include questions and responses. In order to separate\neach section\u2019s key from the others, a parent key named tag\nhas been used. In each key, there are text-based features.\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 128475\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nAlgorithm 1 Pseudocode of the Proposed Chatbot System\nStep 1: Make a json file along with tags, patterns and\nresponse. Here, tags will represent the group, patterns\n\nrepresent the question or query format of the user and\nthe response represents how the bot will respond.\nStep2: load json file, intents\n=json.loads(open(\u2018intents.json\u2019).read()\nStep3: ignore unnecessary letters, ignore_letters= [\u2018?\u2019,\u2019!\u2019]\nstep 4: Store each word of patterns and tags, lemmatize them.\nwords=[lemmatizer.lemmatize(\u2018\u2018word\u2019\u2019) for\n\nword in words if word is not in ignore_letters]\nStep 5: sorts the words for not repeating the same word in\nmodel, words=sorted(set(words))\nStep 6: open the files in binary mode and make dump file for\nit,\n\npickle.dump(words, open(\u2018words.pk1\u2019, \u2019wb\u2019))\npickle.dump(words, open(\u2018class.pk1\u2019,\u2019wb\u2019))\n\nStep7: add all words, patterns after lemmatizing, to training\nlist training= [], empty list\nword_patterns=[lemmatizer.lemmatize(\u2018\u2018word.lower\u2019\u2019()) for\nword in word_patterns], for lemmatizing Loop : word in\nwords list -:(will take each element from words list to word)\n\nbag.append(1) if word in word_patterns or if it is not in\nword_patterns, bag append the value of \u2018\u20180\u2019\u2019\n\noutput_Row=list of output empty, list(output_empty)\noutput_Row of classes.index of documents=1\n\ntraining.append(list of bag and output_Row)\nStep 8: shuffle all words in training list, ran-\ndom.shuffle(training)\nstep 9: store training list one part to train_x and another part\nto train_y\n\ntrain_x=stores list one part,0th index elements, list (:0)\ntrain_y=stores 1st index elements, list (:1)\n\nStep 10: make neural networking by adding Dense and\ndropout to model\n\nmodel=linear stack of layers\n# To add Dense and Dropout\nmodel.add(Dense of (128 and train_x))\nmodel. add (Dropout of 0.6)\nsequential () model. Add (Dense of (train_y[0])\u2019s length)\n\nStep 11: compile it and save the model\nCompile (model, for compiling)\nFit the model, model.fit()\nSave it as.h5 file, save(chatbot_model.h5)\n\nStep 12: after successfully making the training model when-\never a user writes the text on the chatbox, the text will be\nPrediction (message or text)\nstep 13: give reply to the users\n\nresponse = chatbot_response for the message\nchatbot.insert (inserting the \u2018\u2018response\u2019\u2019 to chatbot\n\ninterface)\n\nThese datasets contain different symptoms and diseases such\nas Covid-19, Deep Fever, and chronic diseases. Furthermore,\nthe dataset includes their treatments, methods, RT PCR tests,\n\nStep 14: if the user wants to hear it on voice also\nengine = pyttsx.init()\nengine.say(command)\nengine.runAndWait()\nWhere the command is the bot\u2019s reply, which is con-\n\nverted into the voice\nstep 15: this cycle will continue until the users press exit\nbutton\nstep 16: if the user presses the exit button, the application will\nclose\n\nTABLE 1. Dataset key and contain data type.\n\nside effects, past infections, and more reliable solutions. The\ndataset contained doctor\u2019s numbers, nearby hospitals and\nall needy phone numbers. In addition to detailed treatment\noptions, the user is provided with real-time phone numbers\nof doctors, ambulances, and many other services.\n\nFrom the above TABLE 1, there are some keys categorized\nas parents and some keys used as child. There are more\nparent keys and child keys, more than 20 in number. All\nthe child keys contained text-based data. Whenever a user\nwrites a query, the bot will recognize at which pattern the\nresponses should be given about after creating a successful\nneural network-based model using the above Pattern keys.\nAs a result, it helps to understand the pattern and text for the\nmodel better.\n\nAfter the dataset description, the most critical points that\nshould be addressed as significant topics are the configura-\ntions of the hardware and the software on the system. All\nthe code has been written in python language and text-editors\nare used here spyder vs code. In addition, the application is\nbuilt in Tkinter GUI, which is comfortable for any type of\npc. The test and demo work have been done in mentioned\nconfiguration hardware: -\n\u2022 Intel core i3 10th gen\n\u2022 1 TB HDD\n\u2022 4 GB RAM\n\u2022 Windows 10th operating system\n\nEven in some other operating systems and hardware require-\nments, the application works perfectly.\n\u2022 Ubuntu operating system, Fedora operating system\n\u2022 128 GB SSD\n\u2022 1 GB RAM TO 12GB RAM up to\nThe key model parameters are shown in TABLE 2 above,\n\njust as we mentioned when discussing about the hardware\n\n128476 VOLUME 11, 2023\n\n\n\nS. Furthermore, in order to create\na more biased less model, both parameters kernel_initializer\nand bias_initializer are also set to uniform and zeros.\n\nAs we mentioned model architecture and software-\nhardware configuration, the application operates without a\nhitch in a variety of operating systems and hardware con-\nfigurations due to its lightweight and superior architectural\nmodel. Therefore, we can say almost every device not only\nas exe file even in mobile-app mode the application works\nperfectly. Shows the bot interaction.\n\nB. TRAINING AND TESTING PHASES\nAfter loading the json file and making a training model, the\ntraining model helps the bot to predict the perfect answers\nfor the user. Therefore, before using it in the main file we\nhave to check the training and testing phases and also check\nthe accuracy and loss percentage. The below table is about\nto check the accuracy and a loss percentage of the training\nmodel.\n\nAs we can see in table 1, the accuracy and loss change per\nepoch. It depends on the training model. Here, the accuracy\nincreases as the epoch increases and loss decreases.\n\nFIGURE 11. If the accuracy becomes low or not stable, it causes\nproblems in the bot\u2019s prediction. Meanwhile, some unex-\npected replies will come from the bot when the user asks\nsomething or makes a query. In the above table, the highest\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 128477\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nFIGURE 14. Users ask the bot in voice mode (the asking question is also\nwritten in chatbot, whatever the user says) and the bot reply in both\nmode voice and text.\n\nFIGURE 15. Shows the opening interface to a user along with the chatbot\ninterface.\n\nTABLE 3. Highest accuracy of training model.\n\naccuracy is shown on the epoch 200/200. Here the loss is\na minimum, which is 0.1232 and the highest accuracy is\n94.32%. For getting the results, the algorithm, which is used,\nLSTM has four different components. Forget gate, input gate,\noutput gate, cell gate. All gates have different roles to per-\nform. The forget gate helps to decide what information will\nbe kept or thrown away, and the equation consists of the forget\ngate,\n\nF = \u03c3 (u1X + \u03c91H2) (2)\n\nThe input gate helps to tell what value will be updated, the\nequation which consists of the input gate equation: -\n\nI = \u03c3\n(\nu2X + \u03c92H \u2032\n\n)\n(3)\n\nand the cell gate will combine all forget and input gate\ninformation which is decided to keep and the equation for\nthe cell gate\n\nC = C \u2032F + IG (4)\n\nIn the last output gate will give the output and its equation for\nit O = \u03c3\n\n(\nu4X + \u03c94H \u20322\n\n)\nH \u2032 = Qtan? (C) (5)\n\nOutput Xh\u2032 (6)\n\nIn addition, all these gates are combined and made a perfect\ntrainingmodel containing different loss and accuracy. Fig. 16,\nthe accuracy increases per epoch. However, when the epoch\nis 1/200 to 3/200 the accuracy slowly increases then again in\nepoch 5/200 to 7/200, the accuracy becomes steady, and in\nthe 9/200 to up to last, the accuracy becomes higher than the\nprevious epoch. In the case of test cases, we can see figure 2\nhow the bot starts its interaction with the user. Here, as the\naccuracy of the training model goes well and increases per\nepoch, the user gets a closer reply. If the accuracy becomes\nlow or not proficient, the replies will not be closer or unex-\npected, or unnecessary for the user. Therefore, if we want\na closer prediction, we should maintain the accuracy higher\nand higher. Because higher accuracy makes better proficient,\nneural layers and these layers consist of the data that is\nhelping to build a perfect bot.\n\nFIGURE 16. The accuracy vs epoch graph.\n\nHere, the encoder-decoder configuration, which is hidden,\nplays an important role in the higher accuracy. The informa-\ntion is passed from another layer to another through a repeat-\ning vector layer. To get the loss and accuracy, a bidirectional\nwrapper is added to the LSTM layers. It allows the model for\nboth results and makes a great performance.\n\nAccording to TABLE 4, we can see that the highest accu-\nracy of our model testing is 93.45 and the phase number is 4.\nThat means after 4 phases and many more phases, we got\nthe highest accuracy and perfect reply from our bot. In other\n\n128478 VOLUME 11, 2023\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nTABLE 4. Accuracy testing phase of model.\n\nphrases, the chatbot doesn\u2019t give a close reply to the user,\nand the accuracy after collecting all the information that is\nobtained from the conversion between the chatbot and the\nuser is lower than the others. The testing phase is so important\nbecausewithout this testing phase, we can\u2019t say howmuch the\nbot is going to give a close reply or make good conversions\nwith the user. Even if we implement it in its low accuracy\nstate, the bot will not be helpful for the future user and causes\nproblems when it\u2019s about to give the perfect reply which is\nwanted by the user. Therefore, the testing phase is important\nand should be accurate. Moreover, the accuracy will be higher\nif we test the phase continuously. If we do more testing\nphases, after more phases we can get better accuracy than\nin phase 4. So, if we represent it in the sense of equation\nwe got,\n\nA\u221d T where, T= testing phases and A= accuracy\nA=nT, where n = constant which represents the number\n\nof phases.\nSo, here the accuracy and testing phases are proportionally\n\nthe same. If we do more testing phases, we get more good\naccuracy. And it will increase if the number of testing phases\nincreases.\n\nIn figure 9, it is shown that the accuracy increases during\nthe testing phase. In that case, we can say if we want more\ngood accuracy, we should do more test phases. After all this,\nwe get more goodmodels alongwith good results where users\nalso get the best replies from the bot. In figure 10, how the\ntesting case will look like in each overall accuracy, is shown,\nIn Fig 17, testing cases depend on how the overall accuracy\nlooks like. If the overall accuracy is more proficient than the\nprevious accuracy, the testing cases will also go well and be\nwell predicted. If the accuracy is not better than the previous,\nthe testing cases will not be good. In all test cases, the\ndifferent result outcomes will be determined, and after that,\nthe training model is chosen in which testing cases become\nbetter, as shown in the graph. The layers also consist of good\ndata after collecting the perfect training model. In addition,\nas the training model and layers become more proficient, the\nresults outcomes also become friendly and good for the users\nand also make a great interaction by giving them the right\npredicted replies.\n\nFIGURE 17. Testing phase vs accuracy.\n\nC. TIME COMPLEXITY ANALYSIS\nThe chatbot interface is made in the Tkinter library. For\nloading the library and making it a perfect layout for the bot,\nit takes O(n) time complexity. And for the training model\nand to predict the correct reply to the user\u2019s question, the\nalgorithm\u2019s running time cost O(n), and as we know for\nmaking the training model and putting different layers on it\nto make a perfect neural network, time complexity will be -\nO(4h(3d+h+d)) where the neural network layers d, h and it\nis the proposed time complexity for the LSTM algorithm.\nTherefore, the total time complexity (along with training\nmodel): - O(n+(4h(3d+h+d))) where h, d denotes layers and\nonly for layout and put predict replies,\n\n- Total time complexity: - O(n)\n- Space complexity: - O(n)\n\nFIGURE 18. Accuracy vs testing cases.\n\nAccording to TABLE 5, after performing, different meth-\nods on our chatbot or making the training model using a\ndifferent approach, we can see the different accuracy on each\ndifferent method. Here, LSTM in the case of single-layer\nand for testing and training approaches the time complexity\nwhere, in the case of RNN, the time complexity is the same,\nbut in the case of the decision tree, the time complexity\ndepends on the depth of the tree. Moreover, in the case of\naccuracy, the highest accuracy is from LSTM. The LSTM\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 128479\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nTABLE 5. Accuracy and complexity comparison.\n\nused different gates and also used a gradient to work as a\nneural network, or we can say a better RNN with a sequential\nnetwork. In the case of RNN, it stores sequential input and\nperforms suitable operations when it is needed as sequential\ndata output. In addition, the last decision tree makes different\nnodes and sub-node, and each node performs to store data\nfor users\u2019 use. Here, according to test cases and training\ncases, LSTM, then RNN, and lastly, the Decision Tree got\nthe highest accuracy.\n\nFIGURE 19. Graphical representation of accuracy in different methods.\n\nHowever, according to Fig. 19, we can see that with\ndifferent methods the accuracy increases differently. LSTM\nhas the highest accuracy, whereas RNN is close to LSTM\u2019s\naccuracy. However, the decision tree has the lowest accuracy\namong these three methods. Therefore, we can say that for\nour proposed model and training model, the best approach to\nuse is LSTM. The rest of the two methods are also the best\napproaches to different proposed models. However, as for\nour accuracy in the case of LSTM. Therefore, we choose\nthe LSTM approach to make neural network layers and also\nmake our training model to get the best results in cases of\ninteraction with users and getting the perfect or expected\nreply from a bot.\n\nIn the following table, we have outlined some existing\napproaches to our chatbot. PERSONALIZED CONVERSATIONS\nOur chatbot, Kiwi will provide personalized conversations\nwhere the users will be able to gain information and at the\nsame time, it will feel like a natural experience to them. The\nchatbot will be able to show empathy and be able to perform\npersonalized word choices and adjust its language and tone\nbased on the context of the conversation.\n\nB. OMNI-CHANNEL\nOur chatbot Kiwi can broadcast information across different\nmajor channels including government websites and impor-\ntant organizations, such As WHO which contain relevant\ninformation about Covid 19. If the user provides identifying\ninformation such as the composition of medicines, hospital\nor doctor requirements, the chatbot will provide all the nec-\nessary details and even provide links to websites containing\nthe required information by the user.\n\nC. ACCESSIBILITY\nThe chatbot interface is accessible to all users regardless\nof their platform. The users only need a stable internet\n\n128480 VOLUME 11, 2023\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nconnection to be able to use it. It can read the information\naloud if the user prefers to hear the information which he or\nneeds. It is helpful for the visually impaired as they can speak\nto the chatbot about their queries and the chatbot, in turn,\nspeaks to them and provides important information.\n\nD. CONTEXTUAL ASSISTANCE\nThe chatbot can also be a source of contextual assistance to\nthe health officials and organizations for updating informa-\ntion about Covid 19. We can get a better understanding of the\nproblems, by troubleshooting customer issues and the work\nfor the next updates can be streamlined with the necessary\nimprovements that need to be undertaken.\n\nE. BOOKINGS\nThe chatbot will provide all the necessary information about\nthe availability of hospital beds in an area where the user\nwants the patient to be taken. It would also provide the contact\nnumber for the hospitals and the ambulances, so the booking\nof hospital beds would be easier for the user as they would not\nhave to travel long distances to get a hospital bed. Users can\nalso book an appointment with the doctor if they feel they are\ninfected and need advice right from the safety of their homes.\nThe contact numbers for the doctors will be provided by the\nchatbot and the users don\u2019t have to get out of their homes to\ngather the information.\n\nF. CONVERSATIONAL SERVICE\nThe chatbot will be able to provide conversational service for\nany hospital or health organization by conversing with the\npatients, and it would be helpful in optimizing the treatment\nprocedure based on the reactions of the patients. The medica-\ntions can be selected and the people can also be made aware\nof the dangers posed to them if they fail to take the necessary\nprecautions.\n\nG. STUDENT ASSISTANCE\nStudents conducting research on COVID-19 or the use of\ntechnology in medical science to treat COVID-19 can find\nuseful information from the chatbot. They would find out the\naccuracy level required to maintain a stable conversation and\nprovide the correct information with respect to the queries\ngiven by the user.\n\nVII. BENEFITS\nX 24/7 Support: Chatbots are always at an advantage when\n\nit comes to all-around support even without having to\nscale your team. The chatbot will be available to offer\ninstant and accurate responses around the clock without\nthe need to take any breaks, as is the case with human\ndeployment.\n\nX Save Resources: Chatbots are always useful in saving\nthe time of operators as well as the users. The users\ndo not have to spend their time searching for health\ncenters or hospitals to get information about Covid 19.\n\nIt will also be cost-effective as deploying a chatbot saves\nmoney for any organization since they do not need to pay\nfor the manpower required to maintain the equivalent\nfacilities for the users. It also saves the user money as\nhe does not need to use any transportation and travel\nanywhere to inquire about his needs.\n\nX Reduce Stress for users: It is generally observed that\npatients avoid getting in contact with the customer ser-\nvice of hospitals. They are more comfortable if they are\ntalking to a chatbot and getting their information. The\nsurvey conducted by Help shift discovered that 79% of\npeople prefer live chat to other channels and 55% would\nchoose to use a chatbot if it was available. Chatbots are\nwidely considered to be a quicker and more efficient\nsolution to the users\u2019 problems.\n\nX Handling Information: Chatbots are helpful at handling\nhuge amounts of information and providing the correct\ninformation for the given query without wasting any\ntime. Our chatbot, Kiwi is trained in such a way that it\nhandles all the information given to it seamlessly and\nprovides it as an output to the user\u2019s queries with a high\nrate of accuracy.\n\nFIGURE 20. The chatbot replies regarding medicines that helps the user\nto determine his next course of action.\n\nIn the given Fig. 20, the user asks the chatbot about what\nmedicines he can take if he is infected. The chatbot provides\nall the information about the available medications and thera-\npies for Covid 19 along with their advantages and side effects.\nTherefore, the user gets a full description of his query andwill\nbe able to decide his next course of action after going through\nthe information given to him.\nX Instant response: Chatbots are generally known for their\n\nability to respond to any query immediately, and they can\nhandle queries from thousands of users at the same time\nand provide information to the users immediately. The\nsimultaneous answers provided by our chatbot, Kiwi\nwould also help us to analyze the average response time\nand improve its activities so that the users can get an even\nmore advanced system at their hands.\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 128481\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nVIII. CONCLUSION\nFrom this paper, we can conclude that the chatbot is very\neasy to use for all people; they can use this chatbot in their\nlanguage. This bot offers medical-related information like\ndoctor\u2019s contact details, address of nearby hospitals, contact\ndetails for getting an oxygen cylinder, about the disease its\nsymptoms, its prevalence, diagnosis, and its treatment pro-\ncedures. We consider that our findings will help researchers\nto take advantage of the advanced information in the layout\nand other stuff of these innovative technologies, which may\nbe required for continuous development in the functionality\nof medical chatbots and may help prevent COVID 19. This\nmedical chatbot has wide future opportunities. People in\nremote areas can also receive benefits from this. Here we use\n\u2018TensorFlow\u2019, which helps to build the NLP for chatbots and\nutilizes deep neural network architecture. After building the\nnetwork for our chatbot, it will predict the correct answers\nto the user\u2019s queries. Even if it is not in the training model,\nthe bot tries to predict it closer by checking the sentences\nand their words which will be closer to the training model\u2019s\nresponse.\n\nCOMPLIANCE WITH ETHICAL STANDARDS\nCONFLICT OF INTEREST\nThe authors declare that they have no conflict of interest.\n\nFUNDING\nThere is no external funding involved in this research.\n\nSUPPLEMENTARY\nhttps://github.com/Kodierer-desafiador/Covid-19-dataset-\njson-file\n\nREFERENCES\n[1] J.                 "text": "\ue200filecite\ue202turn0file0\ue201\n\nReceived 17 November 2022, accepted 2 December 2022, date of publication 6 December 2022,\ndate of current version 13 December 2022.\n\nDigital Object Identifier 10.1109/ACCESS.2022.3227208\n\nAn AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for\nInfectious Disease Prediction\nSANJAY CHAKRABORTY1, HRITHIK PAUL 2, SAYANI GHATAK 2, SAROJ KUMAR PANDEY3,\nANKIT KUMAR 3, KAMRED UDHAM SINGH 4, AND MOHD ASIF SHAH 5,6\n1Computer Science and Engineering Department, Techno International Newtown, Kolkata 700156, India\n2Computer Science and Engineering Department, JIS University, Kolkata 700109, India\n3Department of Computer Engineering and Applications, GLA University, Mathura 281406, India\n4Department of Computer Science and Information Engineering, National Cheng Kung University, Tainan 701, Taiwan\n5Department of Economics, Kebri Dehar University, Kebri Dehar 250, Ethiopia\n6School of Business, Woxsen University, Hyderabad 502345, India\n\nCorresponding author: Mohd Asif Shah (drmohdasifshah@kdu.edu.et)\n\nABSTRACT The purpose of this paper is to show concisely how we can promote chatbots in the medical\nsector and cure infectious diseases. We can create awareness through the users and the users can get\nproper medical solutions to prevent disease. We created a preliminary training model and a study report\nto improve human interaction in databases in 2021. Through natural language processing, we describe the\nhuman behaviors and characteristics of the chatbot. In this paper, we propose an AI Chatbot interaction\nand prediction model using a deep feedforward multilayer perceptron. Our analysis discovered a gap in\nknowledge about theoretical guidelines and practical recommendations for creating AI chatbots for lifestyle\nimprovement programs. A brief comparison of our proposed model concerning the time complexity and\naccuracy of testing is also discussed in this paper. In our work, the loss is a minimum of 0.1232 and the\nhighest accuracy is 94.32%. This study describes the functionalities and possible applications of medical\nchatbots and explores the accompanying challenges posed by the use of these emerging technologies during\nsuch health crises mainly posed by pandemics. We believe that our findings will help researchers get a better\nunderstanding of the layout and applications of these revolutionary technologies, which will be required for\ncontinuous improvement in medical chatbot functionality and will be useful in avoiding COVID-19.\n\nINDEX TERMS Artificial intelligence, chatbot, LSTM algorithm, machine learning, natural language\nprocessing, query processing.\n\nI. INTRODUCTION\nCovid-19 is an ailment because of the SARS-CoV-2 virus.\n\u2018World Health Organization (WHO)\u2019 has been declared a\npandemic on March 11, 2020. Around 15 million human\nbeings have been affected worldwide by more than one mil-\nlion deaths by covid-19. From the start, the affordability\nand sustainability of oxygen have been a problem in poor\nand underdeveloped countries. Oxygen is a very important\nmedicine for treating hospitalized patients with Covid-19.\nAccording to PATH, the organization that works with global\ninstitutions and businesses to tackle health problems, the\ndemand for Oxygen cylinders has been growing between\n6%-8% eacdaily India. When someone gets severe Covid-19,\n\nThe associate editor coordinating the review of this manuscript and\n\napproving it for publication was Shadi Alawneh .\n\nthe oxygen levels in the body get low. Patients suffered from\nenormous fevers, coughs, and lost sense of taste and smell, all\nof which became problematic when the SARS-CoV-2 virus\ninvaded them. As a result, to prevent such massive prob-\nlems associated with COVID-19 and facilitate a quick cure\nmethod, we have developed a Chatbot that facility an inter-\nconnection between users and computers in a natural manner.\nIn the twenty-first century, artificial intelligence algorithms\nhave been used to create a revolutionary medium with which\nusers can interact with their needs to prevent and resolve\nacoustic problems easily; one of these is chatbots, a modern\nform of interaction. Chatbot systems use human interactions\nsuch as decision-making performing daily tasks, replying to\nusers quickly, and solving problems like a human. Chatbots\nare also called communicational agents or answering engines.\nDevelopers and programmers train these chatterbots with the\n\nVOLUME 11, 2023 This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License. This application works in a very\nsimple way because the data is already put in the system.\nA few things like matching patterns, NLP (Natural Language\nProcessing), and data mining are used in the application to\ntrain the system. Chatbots match the user input texts or voices\nwith the previously included data and give replies according\nto the data. This knowledge or data has been taken from\nvarious sources. Everything in this generation is getting in\ntouch with the web. It is effective to use a method to manage\nto advantage anything at your doorway. The first established medi-\ncal chatbot \u2013ELIZA\u2013 was programmed in 1966 to simulate\na text-based conversation with a Psychotherapist. The top\nplatform that lends invaluable support to that bot develop-\nment, Amazon Alexa, has more than 0.1 million applications,\nmany of which are for health. The \u2018World Health Organiza-\ntion (WHO) on the renowned social media Facebook Mes-\nsenger to counter wrong information and provide accurate\ninformation about COVID-19 without any delay, launched a\nchatbot. As Chatbots are becoming communicational agent\nin the digital world, it opens many ways for demonstrating\na change in behavior for disease prevention and promoting\nhealth-related actions on a large scale.\n\nThe rest of the paper is organized as follows. Section\n\u2018\u2018Related Work\u2019\u2019 defines some existing state-of-the-art meth-\nods for the AI-assisted Chatbot in health care. Therefore,\nsection-III describes the necessary background of the models\nthat we have used in our research work. The section-IV\ndiscusses the proposed algorithm and overall workflow of the\nproposed AI chatbot system. Then a detailed result analysis\nand a brief comparison among some popular, highly efficient\nmethods are described in section V. Then, a discussion on\nthe applications and benefits of our proposed model is dis-\ncussed in section VI and section VII, respectively. Eventually,\nwe conclude the paper in section VIII by highlighting some\nof the future research scopes.\n\nII. RELATED WORK\nIn the paper [1] the author described the whole procedure\nof developing a chatbot by dividing the process into seg-\nments such as speech-to-text conversion, natural language\nprocessing, response generation, knowledge base creation,\ndialogue management, text-to-speech, etc. The author has\nalso included security considerations such as security flaws\nin chatbot platforms and malicious chatbots. In the paper [3]\nthe authors reviewed topics surrounding the chatbot\u2019s knowl-\nedge domain, response generation, text processing, machine\n\nlearning model, and the dataset usage and evaluation strategy\ntopics. In the paper [4] the authors have described the building\nof a chatbot that can provide an authentic and accurate answer\nfor any type of query using Artificial Intelligence Markup\nLanguage (AIML) and Latent Semantic Analysis (LSA) by\nusing the application of the python platform. In the paper [5]\nthe author covers different approaches to chatbot develop-\nment, including key points on chatbot integration and deploy-\nment, and employs machine learning to configure a chatbot.\nIn the paper [2] the author discussed the method of data anal-\nysis, which allows the analytical system to learn through the\nway of solving problems and applying similar methods in its\nworking process. In the paper [6] the authors have analyzed\nhow artificial intelligence and machine learning are imple-\nmented in popular use to make advancements in chatbot ser-\nvices specifically in helping users to access college websites.\nThe problem selects the closest matching response from the\nclosest matching statement that matches the input and then\nchooses a response from an available selection of statements\nfor that response. In the paper, [7] the author has elaborated on\nthe use of diverse neural framework exhibits as the learning\ntechnique for setting up the chatbot to make it continuously\nlike human enlistment authority. NLP techniques such as\nNLTK (python) can be applied for speech analysis and intel-\nligent responses can be generated by designing a model to\nprovide appropriate human-like responses. In the paper [8]\nthe authors have discussed their research on how to design,\ndevelop and evaluate a health assistant chatbot application\nthat helps users to ask any personal query related to health-\ncare without physically availing any hospital facilities. In the\npaper by [9] the authors have described an AI chatbot whose\nwork depends on Natural language processing. The chatbot\nusers can upload their queries related to healthcare without\nphysically availing of any healthcare facilities. It uses Google\nAPI for voice-text and text-voice conversations. The query is\nsent to the chatbot and the related answer is displayed on an\nandroid app. The system\u2019s main concern behind developing\nthis web-based platform is analyzing customers\u2019 sentiments.\nIn the paper [10] the authors have described a proposed idea to\ncreate a system with AI which meets the user\u2019s requirements.\nThe AI can predict diseases based on symptoms and give\na list of available treatments. The system can also give the\ncomposition of themedicines and their prescribed uses. In our\nchatbot model, we have incorporated the symptoms of Covid\n19 since it is based on only this one disease currently. It also\nshows the list of medications and precautions that the users\nmight take if they are infected. In the paper [11] the authors\nhave focused to show the implementation of a retrieval-based\nchatbot with voice support. They have investigated other\nstanding chatbots and how it is useful in helping the patients\nto fetch all the necessary details about Covid 19. In the\npaper [12] the authors have described the modern chatbot\nfunctioning and incorporating institution-specific responses\nin chatbots related to Covid 19 related queries. The main\nnecessity is for unique response mapping, complex contex-\ntualization, and dynamic validations which are led by human\n\n128470 VOLUME 11, 2023\n\n\n\nS. Chakraborty et al.: AI-Based Medical Chatbot Model for Infectious Disease Prediction\n\nresources of content-led industry leaders to develop a chatbot\nthrough collaborative communication with companies that\nare experienced in machine learning and natural language\nprocessing. They found\nthat interacting with their chatbot for a few minutes sig-\nnificantly increases people\u2019s intention to get vaccinated and\nhave a positive attitude towards Covid 19 vaccination. The\nresults suggest that a properly scripted and regularly updated\nchatbot could offer a powerful resource to help fight hesitancy\ntoward COVID-19 vaccines. Alongwith this, creating a virtual assistant can also\nhelp in the measurement of the severity of the infection and\nconnect with registered doctors when the given symptoms\nbecome serious.
